title: Open Org Profile
description: A self-sovereign organisational profile. The root object representing an organisation in the Open Org standard.

x-minimum-viable-profile.description: The schema validates with just identity.name and mission.summary at the leaf level (everything else in identity and mission is optional). But a minimum viable profile — one that can be discovered, matched, and verified — needs five human-meaningful fields beyond the auto-generated metadata. The agent generates schema_version and updated automatically; the human contributes name, at least one registration number, a primary area of operation, a mission summary, and at least one theme. A charity number alone is enough for the agent to auto-populate four of the five from the public register.

updated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last profile update

identity.description: Core identity facts. The stable information that changes slowly. For a minimum viable profile, provide name, at least one registration number, and a primary area.

name.description: Legal or operating name of the organisation

legal_form.description: Legal structure. Enum covers common UK forms; use 'other' with legal_form_detail for anything else.

legal_form_detail.description: Free text clarification when legal_form is 'other'

registration.description: Regulatory registration numbers. Keys are registry identifiers.

charity_commission_ew.description: Charity Commission for England and Wales number

oscr.description: Office of the Scottish Charity Regulator number

ccni.description: Charity Commission for Northern Ireland number

additionalProperties.description: Other registry numbers not covered above

did.description: Decentralised Identifier for federated protocol use

llms_txt.description: URL to the organisation's llms.txt file

geography.description: Where the organisation operates. Uses ONS geography codes where possible.

primary_area_code.description: ONS geography code for primary area (LAD, LSOA, ward etc.)

operating_area_codes.description: ONS geography codes for operating areas

scale.description: Size indicators. Bands rather than exact figures where appropriate.

staff_count.description: Number of paid staff (FTE or headcount — specify in notes)

founded.description: Date organisation was established (YYYY or YYYY-MM-DD)

mission.description: What the organisation exists to do and how it thinks about change. For a minimum viable profile, provide summary and at least one theme.

summary.description: Plain language mission summary. No jargon. Under 500 characters.

objects.description: Formal charitable objects or constitutional purpose, if different from summary

theory_of_change.description: Reference to a markdown document describing the theory of change

beneficiaries.description: Who the organisation primarily serves, in plain language

themes.description: Thematic tags from the Open Org vocabulary. See /vocabulary/themes.

evidence.description: Evidence of what the organisation has done and learned. Longitudinal — accumulates over time.

governance.description: How decisions are made and who is involved.

agm_date.description: Date of most recent or next AGM

accounts_filed_to.description: Period end date of most recently filed accounts

decision_making.description: How the organisation makes decisions — consent-based, delegated, etc.

culture.description: How the organisation works, what it values, how it treats people. The hardest to structure, the most important to get right.

participation.description: How beneficiaries and communities participate in decision-making

learning_practice.description: How the organisation learns and adapts

values.description: Stated values, in the organisation's own words

services.description: Optional. If the organisation also publishes an Open Referral / HSDS service directory, reference it here rather than duplicating.

hsds_ref.description: URI to the HSDS service record, if one exists

ideas.description: References to published idea objects. Ideas are first-class objects with their own schema.

id.description: Unique identifier for this idea within the organisation

ref.description: URI to the full idea object (org-idea schema)

strategies.description: References to published strategy objects. Strategies are first-class objects — how the organisation thinks about the future, what it has chosen to prioritise, and how its ideas connect.

id.description: Unique identifier for this strategy within the organisation

themes.description: Top-level thematic tags — the primary matching key for cross-org strategy alignment

ref.description: URI to the full strategy object (org-strategy schema)

access.description: Access control configuration for this profile.

public_fields.description: Top-level fields visible without access request. Default: ['identity', 'mission']

request_endpoint.description: Endpoint for funders to request deeper access

extensions.description: Namespace for domain-specific extensions. Keys should be namespaced, e.g. 'uk.housing', 'uk.health'.

narrative_ref.description: Reference to a narrative document. Can be an inline string, a relative path to a markdown file, or an absolute URI.

oneOf.description: Inline text or relative file path (e.g. 'culture/who-we-are.md')

evidence_item.description: A single piece of evidence — an impact report, evaluation, dataset, case study, or learning reflection.

id.description: Unique identifier for this evidence item

summary.description: Brief summary of what this evidence shows

methodology.description: Brief description of how the evidence was gathered

ref.description: Reference to the full evidence document

data_ref.description: URI to structured data (CSV, JSON) if available

access_level.description: Who can see this evidence. 'summary_public' means the summary is visible but full document requires access.
